meta.titleTemplate: Import Coconut Charcoal to the UK: Duty, VAT, Rules
meta.descriptionTemplate: Importing UN 1361 coconut charcoal to the UK from {{port}}: HS 4402.20, 0% duty, 20% VAT with PVA, the CDS entry process, and the FLEGT/REACH position.

h1: Importing coconut charcoal to the United Kingdom
heroSubtitle: The UK import mechanics for UN 1361 coconut charcoal from {{port}}: how it is classified, the duty and VAT, the CDS entry process, and where UK REACH and FLEGT timber rules stand.

hero.ctaQuoteAria: Ask about UK import on WhatsApp
hero.ctaContactAria: Send an inquiry about UK import

intro.p1: This page sits under the {{brand}} Logistics pillar and covers the UK customs mechanics for a container of coconut charcoal. It is the import-procedure guide; the commercial case for the UK market lives on the market page.

keyFacts.heading: UK import at a glance
keyFacts.definition: Classification, headline duty, and who clears the cargo.
keyFacts.docsValue: GB EORI, CDS declaration, S&S (ENS) filing, GMR where GVMS applies
keyFacts.agencyValue: HMRC (customs/VAT); 4402 charcoal not clearly in UKTR/FLEGT scope — confirm with OPSS

hts.body: Coconut-shell charcoal falls under UK heading 4402. The correct, most specific code is 4402 20 00 00 ("of shells or nuts"); 4402 90 00 00 ("other") is the residual timber code and is wrong for this product. Both carry a 0% base rate, so the choice does not change the duty — but declaring 4402.20 signals an agricultural byproduct rather than timber and lowers the chance of a CDS risk-engine hold. Confirm the live line with your customs broker.

duty.body: UK duty on heading 4402 is a short, stable stack: a 0% customs duty layer plus 20% import VAT. Read it as a dated stack with its legal-status flag, and verify the live tariff line at booking.

landed.body: Landed cost is the sum of the goods, the freight, and the import charges. The structure below holds for any lane; the duty and VAT components are public verified figures, while the FOB and freight are illustrative and quoted at booking.
landed.fobValue: Illustrative only — your actual FOB applies
landed.dutyValue: From the stack above (0% duty + 20% import VAT, recoverable via PVA)
landed.note: No charcoal per-ton price is published. The formula is shown so you can plug in your own figures; we quote the live numbers at booking.
landed.rulesLinkLabel: What your incoterm covers (FOB and the freight split)

entry.body: UK entry for a UN 1361 container follows the standard HMRC sequence, handled by the buyer's customs broker with data we supply.

dgHandling.body: The cargo arrives as declared UN 1361, Class {{unClass}} dangerous goods, and is handled accordingly at the UK port. The classification and the prevention controls are covered on the dangerous-goods pages.

countryAgency.h2: UK regulation: REACH, FLEGT timber, and phytosanitary
countryAgency.intro: This is regulatory information, not legal advice — see the disclaimer below.

disclaimer.heading: Regulatory information, not legal advice
disclaimer.body: This page summarizes UK import, REACH, timber (UKTR/FLEGT) and phytosanitary requirements for general information only and is not legal advice. Rules and OPSS/APHA enforcement positions change without notice and are fact-specific. Confirm your obligations with a licensed UK customs broker and, where timber scope is in question, with the OPSS before importing.

responsibilityDestLine: Confirming the UK conformity position — UK REACH reliance on the SDS, and confirming with the OPSS that 4402 coconut-shell charcoal is out of UKTR/FLEGT scope before relying on the exemption

ports.body: Indicative ocean transit from {{portWithCode}} to the main UK gateways:
ports.empty: UK transit times available on request.

items.q: What duty applies to coconut charcoal in the UK?
items.a: The UKGT MFN rate for heading 4402 is 0%. Indonesia is also a DCTS Standard-Preferences country, but since the base rate is already 0% that adds no further benefit and no preference document is needed. Charcoal is not in Indonesia's graduated chapters, and Indonesia's full DCTS graduation from 1 January 2027 leaves the duty at 0%. Verify the live tariff line with your broker at booking.

Yes. Import VAT is 20% on the customs value plus duty plus freight, insurance and incidental UK charges. A VAT-registered importer can use Postponed VAT Accounting (PVA) to account for it on the VAT return rather than paying at the border, and recover it as input tax. The reduced 5% fuel rate is for sub-1-tonne domestic supply and does not apply to a commercial container.

items.q: Do UK timber or FLEGT rules apply to coconut charcoal?
items.a: Probably not. The OPSS UKTR enforcement report checks 4401/4407/4409/4412 timber codes, not 4402 charcoal, and coconut-shell charcoal is an agricultural byproduct of the coconut palm, not timber. Indonesia is the only country with a UK FLEGT VPA, so genuinely in-scope timber would need a FLEGT licence — but carbonised coconut charcoal is not clearly in scope. Confirm with the OPSS before relying on the exemption. UK REACH applies via the SDS we provide.

items.q: How long does shipping from Indonesia to the UK take?
items.a: We model the lane ex-{{portWithCode}} to the main UK gateways — Felixstowe, London Gateway and Southampton — at roughly the transit shown in the list above. Other UK ports are available; confirm routing and the sailing schedule with the carrier at booking. Class 4.2 dangerous-goods bookings need extra lead time, so book early.
